BiomeDef.label: Freezing Wasteland
BiomeDef.description: A barren, frozen wasteland. Bitter cold, with little life. Soil is mostly sand, with sparse patches of soil and very little fertile soil. Vegetation and animals are as scarce as in the desert, but what survives is similar to the scarlands.

BiomeDef.label: Wasteland
BiomeDef.description: A temperate wasteland. Harsh, dry, and mostly barren. Soil and life as in the desert, but temperature is moderate.

BiomeDef.label: Scorching Wasteland
BiomeDef.description: An extremely hot, dry wasteland. Life is rare, and the ground is mostly sand and rock. Vegetation and animals are as in the scarlands, but densities are as low as the desert.

BiomeDef.label: Central Valley
BiomeDef.description: A vast, open grassland with almost no trees. Temperate to hot, with droughts like grassland.

BiomeDef.label: Brahmin Woods
BiomeDef.description: Grassland with more trees. Temperate to hot, drought-prone like grassland.

BiomeDef.label: Sea Ice
BiomeDef.description: Permanent ice sheets floating on water. There is no soil for plants to grow, no minerals to mine, and almost no animal life.
BiomeDef.settleWarning: Sea ice is extremely inhospitable. There is no soil for plants and almost no animal life. Long-term survival is a challenge.

BiomeDef.label: Ice Sheet
BiomeDef.description: Sheets of ice which can be kilometers thick. There is no soil for plants to grow in. The only animals here are migrating to somewhere else - or badly lost.
BiomeDef.settleWarning: Ice sheet is extremely inhospitable. There is no soil for plants and almost no animal life. Long-term survival is a challenge.

BiomeDef.label: Tundra
BiomeDef.description: These mostly-frozen plains bear almost no trees and little vegetation. There are a few small animals interspersed with large herds of migratory grazers and their predators.

BiomeDef.label: Redwood
BiomeDef.description: Dense, ancient redwood forest. Cold, but not as harsh as boreal forest.

BiomeDef.label: Sequoia
BiomeDef.description: Massive sequoia trees in a temperate climate.

BiomeDef.label: Divide
BiomeDef.description: Mountainous temperate forest, high elevation.

BiomeDef.label: Monte
BiomeDef.description: Lush, dense rainforest.

BiomeDef.label: Arroyo
BiomeDef.description: Wet, tropical swamp.

BiomeDef.label: Sagebrush
BiomeDef.description: Dry, shrubby brushland.

BiomeDef.label: Crater
BiomeDef.description: A blasted crater. Rare, small biome.
BiomeDef.settleWarning: Scarlands are inhabited by dangerous insects and rogue mechanoids. Water is toxic and scaria runs rampant among the animals.

BiomeDef.label: Scorched Forest
BiomeDef.description: Burned, glowing forest. Rare, small biome.
BiomeDef.settleWarning: Large sulfur spouts permanently darken the skies in glowforests, making it hard to grow most crops.

BiomeDef.label: Glacier
BiomeDef.description: Massive glacier. Rare, small biome.

BiomeDef.label: Malpais
BiomeDef.description: Volcanic badlands. Rare, small biome.
BiomeDef.settleWarning: This area has high volcanic activity. It is threatened by clouds of volcanic ash, as well as periodic lava eruptions.

BiomeDef.label: Canyon
BiomeDef.description: Hot canyon, mostly soil and stone, rare, small biome.

BiomeDef.label: Spring
BiomeDef.description: Hot canyon with lakes, rare, small biome.

BiomeDef.label: Redwood Lake
BiomeDef.description: Redwood forest with lakes, rare, small biome.

BiomeDef.label: Sequoia Lakes
BiomeDef.description: Sequoia forest with lakes, rare, small biome.

BiomeDef.label: Sanctuary
BiomeDef.description: Temperate forest sanctuary, rare, small biome.

BiomeDef.label: Oasis
BiomeDef.description: Temperate forest oasis with lakes, rare, small biome.

BiomeDef.label: Slough
BiomeDef.description: A cold, wet, and marshy biome with thick mud, stunted trees, and frequent fog. Similar to the vanilla bog, but with a unique name and minor flavor differences.
